message:
refactor(layers): align skill layer terminology to base/org/project/user

Skill Layer Terminology Alignment
==================================

This commit standardizes the skill layer naming convention across the entire
codebase. The previous terminology (system/global/project/local) was inconsistent
with the documented architecture and caused confusion. The new terminology
(base/org/project/user) better reflects the layering hierarchy:

  base (lowest) → org → project → user (highest)

This aligns with the README documentation and makes the override semantics
clearer: higher layers override lower layers.

Changes in src/core/skill.rs:
- Add SkillLayer::as_str() method returning &'static str for each variant
- Implement std::fmt::Display for SkillLayer using as_str()
- These additions enable ergonomic layer stringification throughout the codebase

Changes in src/core/mod.rs:
- Re-export SkillLayer from the core module public API

Changes in src/core/disclosure.rs:
- Add "moderate" as an alias for DisclosureLevel::Standard
- This provides flexibility in how users specify disclosure levels

Changes in src/search/context.rs:
- Rename SearchLayer variants: System→Base, Global→Org, Local→User
- Update from_str() to accept both old and new names for backwards compat:
  * "system" | "base" → Base
  * "global" | "org" → Org
  * "local" | "user" → User
- Update as_str() to return new canonical names
- Fix layer matching in SearchFilters::matches() to normalize layer strings
  before comparison, handling both old and new terminology
- Update all tests to use new terminology

Changes in src/search/filters.rs:
- Update test fixtures to use "org" instead of "global"

Changes in src/search/tantivy.rs:
- Update normalize_layer() function to return new canonical names
- Maintain backwards compatibility by accepting old names as input

The backwards compatibility ensures existing databases and configurations
continue to work while new code uses the standardized terminology.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -57,7 +57,7 @@ impl DisclosureLevel {
         match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {
             "minimal" | "0" => Some(DisclosureLevel::Minimal),
             "overview" | "1" => Some(DisclosureLevel::Overview),
-            "standard" | "2" => Some(DisclosureLevel::Standard),
+            "standard" | "moderate" | "2" => Some(DisclosureLevel::Standard),
             "full" | "3" => Some(DisclosureLevel::Full),
             "complete" | "4" => Some(DisclosureLevel::Complete),
             "auto" => Some(DisclosureLevel::Auto),
